comments.post_body:
I am grateful to take on the role of Principal Group Engineering Manager at Microsoft.
This next chapter is focused on bridging enterprise customers and Windows product engineering, accelerating recovery from critical issues and converting escalations and real-world customer experiences into durable, shipped product improvements.
Thankful for the trust and for the exceptional group of engineers I get to work with every day, who continue to raise the bar and make this work possible.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is expressing gratitude for their new role as Principal Group Engineering Manager at Microsoft, where they will focus on improving Windows product engineering and customer experiences. They are thankful for the trust and opportunity to work with a talented group of engineers.

comments.post_body:
#CareerUpdate
Dear SAP 


I want to take a moment and thank you with all my heart for providing me an incredible opportunity to work with you. I was a developer in PLM core - LDM in private cloud products. With my 4.5 years, I can proudly say I loved every moment both personally and professionally which helped me grow as a professional developer.

#2025 has been an incredible year with a lot of contribution and recognitions. Years before weren't any less. With right opportunity and a rewarding atmosphere is what every developer seeks.

As I say a tough good-bye to SAP and all my colleagues, i will always look back and remember these years. Amazing opportunities, experiences, memories, emotions, contributions, rewards and recognitions and most of all the homely atmosphere... Thank you SAP for this incredible and an unforgettable journey.


#awards #CareerUpdate #Greatful 
#NextChapter #HeartfeltGoodbye
comments.context_summary: The commenter is expressing gratitude to SAP for the opportunity to work with them as a developer and is bidding a heartfelt goodbye after 4.5 years, reflecting on the incredible experiences and growth they've had. They're thankful for the rewarding atmosphere and are now moving on to their next chapter.

comments.post_body:
𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐲 𝐜𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐥𝐲 𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐝 𝐚 𝐧𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐅𝐘 2018–19 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐧𝐨𝐧-𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐀𝐬𝐬𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞.

What’s alarming?
 👉 Dividend income from a foreign investment was not reported, and the department has invoked the Black Money (Undisclosed Foreign Income and Assets) Act.

𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐲 𝐭𝐚𝐱𝐩𝐚𝐲𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭:
Old years are “closed”
Small foreign dividends don’t matter
AIS is not showing the same
That assumption is costly.

Foreign assets, even with minor income, must be disclosed every year once acquired. Non-disclosure can trigger severe penalties, irrespective of the amount involved.

📌 Key takeaway:
 If you have ever held foreign shares, ESOPs, RSUs, ETFs, bank accounts, or earned foreign dividends — review your past filings now, not after a notice arrives.
Compliance delayed is often compliance denied.

𝐍𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫 𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 10(1) 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐁𝐥𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐲 (𝐔𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐀𝐬𝐬𝐞𝐭𝐬) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 𝐓𝐚𝐱 𝐀𝐜𝐭, 2015

Stay safe. Stay disclosed.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about a client who received a notice for non-disclosure of foreign assets and income, highlighting the importance of reporting all foreign assets and income, no matter how small, to avoid severe penalties. The post warns taxpayers to review their past filings to ensure compliance with the Black Money Act and tax laws.

comments.comment_body: Ravi Gopinath In case of foreign assets income tax can go back for 16 years also to trigger the provisions of black money act

What’s alarming?

𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐲 𝐭𝐚𝐱𝐩𝐚𝐲𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭:
Old years are “closed”
Small foreign dividends don’t matter
AIS is not showing the same
That assumption is costly.

📌 Key takeaway:

Compliance delayed is often compliance denied.

𝐍𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫 𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 10(1) 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐁𝐥𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐲 (𝐔𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐀𝐬𝐬𝐞𝐭𝐬) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 𝐓𝐚𝐱 𝐀𝐜𝐭, 2015

comments.post_body:
Got my 5 year anniversary crystal today.

While choosing the crystal, I was musing over whether it would be practical to just take the cash reward. It felt a bit silly to attach so much value to an object. Aren’t moments and experiences supposed to matter more than souvenirs?

Fast forward to unboxing the crystal today, and it sent me straight down memory lane. Building the Connections app from scratch, the 1.5M celebration, singing harmonies at the gala evening, so many team outings, and now contributing to next‑gen SharePoint – the past 5 years have been full of learning and fun moments. I’ve been lucky to be part of a team that celebrates each other so much.

Huge gratitude to Amit Dhodapkar, Ashish Singhal, Snehdip Karandikar, Venu Sanda, Vinit Baheti and my entire team for these last 5 years.

Glad I chose the crystal – I've realized that it is not just a keepsake, rather a physical reminder of five years of work, growth and memories. Almost like a little memoire sitting on my desk.
Looking forward to what’s next!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is celebrating their 5-year work anniversary and reflecting on the value of a crystal gift, realizing it serves as a meaningful reminder of their experiences and memories over the past five years. They express gratitude to their team and look forward to what's next in their career.

comments.post_body:
🚀 𝗜 𝗮𝗺 𝘀𝘂𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝗲𝘅𝗰𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗼 𝗮𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗜 𝘄𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗯𝗲 𝗷𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 #𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮𝗥𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁 𝗮𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗣 𝗼𝗳 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺 𝘁𝗲𝗮𝗺.

🔙 𝗟𝗼𝗼𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗯𝗮𝗰𝗸....
... when I joined UiPath, it had an amazing technology that enterprises were excited about because it held immense promise to transform how they operate in positive disruptive ways. To unlock that promise, we needed to make UiPath enterprise-ready, scalable, and cloud-native.

Over the past 7 years, we went well beyond that, we delivered the most comprehensive enterprise governance and automation capabilities in the market in a way that supported not just RPA, but a unified suite of products working together as part of an integrated platform, trusted by some of the most complex and regulated enterprises and public sector globally.

The recent release of UiPath Act2 agentic capabilities directly integrated into the platform is a milestone that captures the flexibility and power of the UiPath platform, enabling customers to manage agents just like robots, but also accelerating the pace at which new innovations can be delivered internally in UiPath as well as for customers.

None of this would have been possible without the brilliant, and collaborative people I had the privilege of working with across multiple teams and disciplines. Thank you to everyone who leaned in, stood up for the customer, challenged and listened, and helped turn vision into execution.

A special shout-out, and, many, many, many thanks to the platform team, the engine behind the scenes that makes things "just work". Success for us was making things invisible :), ensuring everything felt seamless, integrated, and frictionless for the user while offering the control and governance enterprises expect. When it all just worked, it meant we did our job right.
🙏 It was a privilege and a pleasure working with you all.

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁’𝘀 𝗻𝗲𝘅𝘁: 𝗜’𝗺 𝗶𝗻𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗯𝗹𝘆 𝗲𝘅𝗰𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗼 𝗯𝗲 𝗷𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮𝗥𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁 to help accelerate this rocket ship. From my very first conversations with Venky, Prajakta, and others on the leadership team, I was immediately drawn in by the team's thoughtfulness, visionary leadership, compelling ideas, and how naturally engaging every conversation felt. I knew right then that I wanted to join them and help tackle those challenges together.

♥️ UiPath and the people I worked with will always hold a special place in my heart, you all helped me grow and learn in ways I didn't imagine before. Can’t list you all, you know who you are 😊.
Thank you Daniel Dines for getting me onboard 7 years ago!

🚀 Prajakta Damle, Dinek Kothottil, Venky Veeraraghavan and everyone at #DataRobot I am so excited to join and looking forward to work together!
Thank you for getting me onboard! #𝗻𝗲𝘄𝗷𝗼𝗯
comments.context_summary: The commenter is excited to announce they will be joining DataRobot as the VP of product for the platform team, and is reflecting on their past 7 years at UiPath where they helped deliver comprehensive enterprise governance and automation capabilities. They express gratitude to their former colleagues and are looking forward to their new role at DataRobot.

comments.post_body:
Today marks 25 years at Microsoft — a journey that’s been both humbling and deeply fulfilling.  What started as an opportunity to work on personal finance products turned into a lifelong journey of learning, building, and growing across continents, technologies, and people.

Over these years, I’ve had the privilege of contributing to products that touched millions from Financial Products to MSN Core Platform, Bing Search, Microsoft AI, and now Azure Cloud. Along the way, I’ve been part of teams that shaped the evolution of the web, cloud, and AI platforms that define Microsoft today.

My journey has taken me from Redmond to India, with stops in Japan and Singapore. Through this journey, I’ve had the privilege to work with incredible people - colleagues, mentors, and partners, who inspired me every single day.

I’ve seen Microsoft reinvent itself many times and through each reinvention, I’ve grown too. The constant has been the people: curious, passionate, and deeply collaborative, which is why Microsoft remains such a special place for me!

I’m deeply grateful for the opportunities, the learning, and the trust placed in me through the years.  Thank you to each person that’s been part of my journey - each of you have helped shape me and for that I’m always grateful. Wishing each of you well wherever you are! Here’s to the next chapter of growth, innovation, and purpose. 💙

#Microsoft #25Years #Gratitude #Leadership #Azure #AI #Engineering
comments.context_summary: The commenter is celebrating their 25-year milestone at Microsoft, reflecting on their journey and expressing gratitude for the opportunities and people they've encountered along the way. They're thanking everyone who has been part of their journey and looking forward to the next chapter of growth and innovation.

comments.post_body:
After more than 15 years at Microsoft, I made the leap from corporate leadership into full-time entrepreneurship — and what a journey it has been!
I’m proud to share that over the last few years, I’ve transitioned from building global cloud infrastructure systems to building businesses that serve families, communities, and investors.

Over the past several years, I’ve grown a diverse portfolio of ventures, including: Food, Child Care and Real Estate syndication businesses.

What started as a career shift has turned into a passion for scaling businesses, developing teams, and giving back to the community. Along the way, our companies have raised tens of thousands for causes like Make-A-Wish and Best Buddies — a reminder that success is best when it’s shared.

I’m deeply and forever grateful for the experiences and mentorship I had at Microsoft, and even more excited about this next chapter of growth and impact as an entrepreneur.

Here’s to building, scaling, and serving communities in new ways!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about the author's transition from corporate leadership at Microsoft to full-time entrepreneurship, where they've built a diverse portfolio of ventures and are passionate about scaling businesses and giving back to the community. The commenter is likely congratulating or showing support for the author's new journey as an entrepreneur.

comments.post_body:
Today (10/4) marks 15 years since #MyMicrosoftStory started. 
 
It’s not all magic — but it’s still meaningful. Started out as a dev, tried out PM and found my calling. Backend, frontend, platform, dev community - done it all and love doing it. And I have #SharePoint to thank for it all. 

Here's to the next 15 years of building, doubting, growing, repeating.

#InvestedAndInspired

comments.post_body:
I’m happy to share that I’m starting a new position as Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft!

What a journey it’s been since November 2015 — from Cortana 🤖, Bing Local 📍, Bing Shopping 🛒, Authentication 🔐, Homepage 🏠, to Enterprise Search 🔎 and now Copilot 🤝.  

I’ve had the privilege of working on latest technologies that impact billions of users 🌍 worldwide — from personal assistants 🗣 to consumer search products 🔍 to enterprise-grade search 🏢, and now the transformative world of AI 🤖 and LLMs 📚 in productivity.  

Grateful 🙏 to have worked with incredibly smart teammates 👩‍💻👨‍💻 and supportive colleagues throughout this journey. Every challenge has been a learning opportunity 📖, and every milestone a step forward 🛤.  

Here’s to building the future with purpose and passion. 🚀
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new position as Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft, responding to the author's announcement about their new role and reflecting on their journey since 2015. The author is expressing gratitude for their experiences and looking forward to building the future with purpose and passion.

comments.post_body:
Congratulations to Nikhil Raj on his appointment as Chief Executive Officer of MetaRouter! 

With over two decades of leadership across Walmart, Bain & Company, and Moloco, Nikhil has been at the forefront of retail media, data, and AI transformation. He co-founded Walmart Connect, launched Bain’s global retail media practice, and helped scale Moloco into a global commerce media powerhouse.

Sharing his vision, Nikhil said: “MetaRouter has enabled several Fortune 100 enterprises to take control of their customer data. It is an absolute honor and privilege to partner with the team to take MetaRouter to the next level.”

Tim Brunk, MetaRouter co-founder, added: “Nikhil’s journey is MetaRouter — his time at Walmart, Bain, and Moloco has shaped the very approach we take to building secure data infrastructure.”

Wishing Nikhil Raj great success in this new role! 

Read The Full Story Here:https://lnkd.in/ecupd4_G

Subscribe to HR TODAY: https://lnkd.in/dxKmqXqn
(𝐆𝐥𝐨𝐛𝐚𝐥 𝐍𝐮𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐉𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐨𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩 𝐄𝐧𝐠𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭)

comments.post_body:
Excited to share that I’ve officially completed my Master’s in Computer Science from Georgia Institute of Technology, with a specialization in Interactive Intelligence 🎓💡.

Over the past three years, I explored a wide range of subjects like Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing, AI for Robotics, Video Game Design, AI etc. From experimenting with algorithms to training (sometimes endlessly, sometimes successfully 😅), building models, writing reports to building video games. Every course pushed me to think creatively while honing both my technical skills and problem-solving mindset.

Balancing a demanding graduate program with full-time work wasn’t always easy - late-night projects, weekend sprints, and rolling straight into Monday meetings, but it was absolutely worth it. I’m especially grateful to my parents, GaTech faculty & peers and my managers - Saradhi, Ramesh and Karan for their support throughout.

Looking forward to applying these skills to build innovative solutions and take on exciting, real-world challenges.

Here’s to the next chapter and all the opportunities it brings! 🚀
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on completing their Master's in Computer Science from Georgia Institute of Technology, with a specialization in Interactive Intelligence, and is likely responding to the author's post about their academic achievement and future plans. The author's post shares their excitement and gratitude for completing their degree, and their enthusiasm for applying their new skills to real-world challenges.

comments.post_body:
It’s been a wild couple of months  - new role, big vision, and a ton of learning already!
I’ve joined Extreme Networks as Director of Product – AI.
Extreme isn’t interested in being a networking company experimenting with AI. 
We want to be an AI company that completely redefines networking.
I’m here to shape that future, and I’m thrilled for the ride.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is excited to share their new role as Director of Product – AI at Extreme Networks, where they aim to redefine networking with AI technology. They're looking forward to shaping the company's future in this space.

I'm thrilled to share that I've joined Redwood Software as VP of Engineering and India Site Leader.

Redwood Software is a pioneer in enterprise orchestration and workload automation, helping global organizations streamline mission-critical processes across hybrid and cloud environments. It’s an exciting space at the intersection of automation, AI, and enterprise reliability — and I’m energized to help shape what’s next.

Looking forward to this new chapter and the opportunity to work with exceptional teams as we scale and innovate.
#RedwoodSoftware #VPofEngineering #SiteLeader #Automation #AI #EngineeringLeadership #EnterpriseSoftware

comments.post_body:
I’m thrilled to share that I am joining Amazon as an SDE-2!

After three great years at Microsoft and a roller-coaster of interviews—some wins, some lessons—I’m humbled and excited to join Amazon as an SDE-2. Sharing this moment with my parents at the Amazon office was unforgettable—nothing beats seeing them proud. 

To everyone who cheered me on and believed in me—thank you from the bottom of my heart. Your belief in me made all the difference. And to those still hustling— stay consistent, trust your path, and never give up. Your moment will come! 

Glory to Lord Krishna for all the strength, silent guidance, and blessings throughout this journey.

#amazon #bengaluru
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new role as an SDE-2 at Amazon, after the author shared their exciting news and expressed gratitude to those who supported them throughout their journey. The commenter is likely responding to the author's heartfelt post and celebrating their achievement.

comments.comment_body: Ravi Gopinath Thanks a ton Ravi for the guidance and support during my tenure at Microsoft. It was great working with you!

comments.post_body:
I’m honored to be included in the 2025 Ad Age Tech Power List, which recognizes leaders using emerging technology to meaningfully transform marketing, media, and advertising.
 
At Microsoft, I have the privilege of leading an incredible global team across advertising, MSN, and shopping. Together, we have delivered 100s of updates to the product — from evolving ad formats to integrating Copilot across surfaces.
 
One of the most exciting areas of our work is intelligent brand agents. These aren’t hypothetical — they’re live pilots built to help brands engage customers in new ways, shaped by real-world insight and designed for the future of agent-to-agent marketplaces.
 
Thank you to Ad Age for recognizing this work, and to my teams and partners for your vision and commitment to building the future for our customers.

 https://lnkd.in/gc2xt2kr
 
#AdAgeTechPowerList #AI #MicrosoftAdvertising
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about being included in the 2025 Ad Age Tech Power List, which recognizes leaders in emerging technology for marketing, media, and advertising, and is thanking Ad Age and their teams for the recognition. The post highlights the commenter's work at Microsoft, particularly in intelligent brand agents and other innovative advertising technologies.

comments.post_body:
After an incredible 5-year journey at Microsoft, it’s time for a new chapter. I’m filled with gratitude for the experiences, the challenges, and above all, the amazing people I had the privilege to work with and learn from.
A heartfelt thank you to my manager and mentor, Kulo Rajasekaran for his unwavering support, inclusive leadership, and the strong culture he fosters — it made all the difference. ODSP is a great place to build with purpose and impact, and I'm proud to have been part of that journey.
To my peers, partners, and teammates: thank you for making this journey so meaningful.
I’m now excited to be starting a new chapter, where I’ll continue to build and scale with purpose. Looking forward to what’s ahead!
#Gratitude #NewBeginnings #MicrosoftAlumni #Leadership #Onward
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about someone leaving Microsoft after a 5-year journey, expressing gratitude for their experiences and colleagues. The original poster is now starting a new chapter and is excited for what's ahead, prompting the commenter to respond to their announcement.

comments.comment_body: Is fatty level disease reversible? What can be done to reverse it? Dr. Manan Vora
comments.post_body:
84% of IT employees in India have a damaged liver — and most don’t even know it.

A new study from the University of Hyderabad confirms Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) is now an epidemic in India’s IT sector:

- 84% of IT employees are already affected.
- 71% are clinically obese.
- 34% have metabolic syndrome (diabetes, high BP, abnormal cholesterol)

This is a lifestyle disease, and you should be concerned if you follow these habits:

🚩 Sitting 8+ hours daily

🚩 Sleeping <6 hours everyday

🚩 Consuming 2 sugary drinks/week (+45% risk)

🚩 Increasing belly fat - Waist over 90 cm (men) or 80 cm (women)

Fatty liver progresses silently until it's too late, potentially leading to:

- Liver inflammation → Chronic damage
- Fibrosis → Scarring of liver tissue
- Cirrhosis → Permanent damage requiring a liver transplant
- Liver cancer → The final, deadly stage

As a doctor, I wish more people realise this — Your deadlines, meetings, and late-night grind WILL NOT matter if your health fails.

So fix it before it’s too late:

▶︎ 1. Get a liver check-up (ultrasound & LFT detect early damage)

▶︎ 2. Avoid sugar & refined carbs when you can (Moderation is key)

▶︎ 3. Move every hour (even 5-minute breaks help)

▶︎ 4. Prioritise protein & fibre (helps burn excess liver fat)

▶︎ 5. Fix your sleep (sleep deprivation damages liver more than alcohol)

Most people only realise the value of health when they lose it. But don't wait until your body forces you to stop.

If you found this helpful, please repost 🔁 this. Someone in your network needs to read this today.

#healthandwellness #workplacehealth #fitness
